messages.content:
suddenly with no reason after the project was worked successfully, i tried to serve again give me this D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\webpack-sources\lib\SizeOnlySource.js:16
                return new Error(
                       ^

Error: Content and Map of this Source is not available (only size() is supported)
    at SizeOnlySource._error (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\webpack-sources\lib\SizeOnlySource.js:16:10)
    at SizeOnlySource.buffer (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\webpack-sources\lib\SizeOnlySource.js:30:14)
    at _isSourceEqual (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\@angular-devkit\build-angular\node_modules\webpack\lib\util\source.js:21:51)
    at isSourceEqual (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\@angular-devkit\build-angular\node_modules\webpack\lib\util\source.js:43:17)
    at Compilation.emitAsset (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\@angular-devkit\build-angular\node_modules\webpack\lib\Compilation.js:4253:9)
    at D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\@angular-devkit\build-angular\node_modules\webpack\lib\Compiler.js:566:28
    at D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\@angular-devkit\build-angular\node_modules\webpack\lib\Compiler.js:1200:17
    at eval (eval at create (D:\projects\oms_admin\node_modules\tapable\lib\HookCodeFactory.js:33:10), <anonymous>:13:1)
    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at runNextTicks (node:internal/process/task_queues:64:3)

It seems like there is an error related to the webpack-sources module in your project. The error message indicates that the content and map of the source are not available, and only the size() method is supported. 

To troubleshoot this issue, you can try the following steps:
1. Check if there are any recent changes in your project that might have caused this error.
2. Make sure that all dependencies are up to date, especially webpack-sources.
3. Verify that the source files are correctly referenced in your webpack configuration.
4. Look for any other error messages in your console that could provide more information about the root cause of the problem.

If the issue persists, you may need to delve deeper into the webpack configuration and debugging to pinpoint the exact cause of the error.
this function doesn't show the result when i search

If your function is not displaying the expected results when searching, there may be an issue with how the filtering is being applied. Here are a few things you can check and adjust in your `transform` function to ensure the search functionality works correctly:

1. Make sure the `input` value is correctly passed to the function and that it is being used for filtering.

2. Verify that the `value` being passed to the function is an array of strings or objects with string properties that you want to search through.

3. Double-check the filter condition to ensure it correctly filters based on the search criteria.

4. Verify that the `transform` function is properly integrated with the component or service that uses it for displaying search results.

If you are still facing issues with the search functionality, you may need to debug the function by adding console logs to see the values of `value`, `input`, and `val` inside the filter function to understand how the filtering is being applied and troubleshoot the issue further.
return value.filter((val:any) => val?.toLowerCase().indexOf(input.toLowerCase()) >= 0);  ERROR TypeError: val?.toLowerCase is not a function
i have 30 items by your answer the length set to 0

If you are encountering issues with getting the correct length of the `ordersList`, you may need to ensure that the length is accurately calculated before using it to set the `pageSize`. Here are some steps you can take to ensure the correct length is used:

1. Verify Data: Double-check that the `ordersList` is populated with the correct data before trying to get its length. Make sure the data is successfully retrieved and assigned to `ordersList` from the API response.

2. Debug Length: Before setting the `pageSize`, you can debug and log the length of the `ordersList` to see if it is calculated correctly. For example, you can add a `console.log(this.ordersList.length)` statement just before setting the `pageSize`.

3. Synchronize Data Updates: Ensure that any updates to the `ordersList` are reflected synchronously. If data is being loaded asynchronously, make sure that setting the `pageSize` occurs after the data has been fully loaded into the `ordersList`.

4. Handle Asynchronous Issues: If the data loading process is asynchronous, consider handling the setting of `pageSize` within the subscription block of the API call to ensure the orders data has been fully loaded before setting the `pageSize`.

By following these steps and ensuring that the `ordersList` is correctly populated and its length is accurately calculated before setting the `pageSize`, you should be able to get the correct length and dynamically adjust the `pageSize` based on the number of orders retrieved.
